title: Working with Anger
description: From the spiral of obsession and anger toward composure that does not add turbulence.
entry_queries: I get angry and can't think
how do I stop being so angry

steps.practice: When obsessing on a person, object, or outcome, name the chain: dwell → attach → crave → anger.
steps.reflection: What am I repeatedly dwelling on that is quietly building anger?

steps.practice: Use a cool-down: leave the room, drink water, write the angry sentence privately before speaking.
steps.reflection: Where has anger already started erasing my memory of what matters?

steps.practice: Before reacting to criticism or praise, pause one breath and ask: will my response add turbulence?
steps.reflection: Do I unsettle others more than I realize — and do I let others unsettle me more than I need to?
